title: Dose-Esc/Exp RMC4630 & Cobi in Relapsed/Refractory Solid Tumors & RMC4630& Osi in EGFR+ Locally Adv/Meta NSCLC

eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures eligibility for the trial and is a standard inclusion criteria.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Cobimetinib only - Participants who have advanced solid tumors that have failed, are intolerant to, or are considered ineligible for standard of care anti-cancer treatments including approved drugs for oncogenic drivers in their tumor type.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding treatment eligibility.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Osimertinib only - Locally advanced or metastatic EGFR mutant NSCLC not amenable to curative surgery or radiotherapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with statistical power concerns regarding disease severity.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Cobimetinib only - Participants must have one of the following genotypic aberrations: KRAS mutations and amplifications, BRAF Class 3 mutations, or NF1 LOF mutations
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with statistical power concerns regarding disease severity.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Osimertinib only - Evidence of radiological documentation of progression with osimertinib monotherapy or an osimertinib containing regimen. Participants should not be considered a current candidate for 1st generation EGFR TKI's by the investigator.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with statistical power concerns regarding disease progression.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of ≤1
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding patient health.

eligibility.text: Adequate hematological, hepatic, and renal function
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with feasibility concerns regarding patient health.

eligibility.text: Capable of giving signed informed consent form (ICF). Willing and able to compile with study requirements and restrictions
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with feasibility concerns regarding patient understanding.
This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding patient health.

eligibility.text: Female of childbearing potential and males with partners of childbearing potential must comply with effective contraception criteria.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with feasibility concerns regarding pregnancy.
This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding CNS health.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding neurological health.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + osimertinib arm only - Known or suspected Small cell, squamous, or pleomorphic lung transformations
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding lung health.
This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding cardiovascular health.

eligibility.text: Active, clinically signiﬁcant interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding respiratory health.

eligibility.text: History or current evidence of retinal pigment epithelial detachment (RPED), central serous retinopathy, retinal vein occlusion, or predisposing factors to RPED or RVO
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding eye health.

eligibility.text: Known HIV infection or active/chronic hepatitis B or C infection.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding immune system health.

eligibility.text: Any other unstable or clinically signiﬁcant concurrent medical condition that would, in the opinion of the investigator, jeopardize the safety of a participant, impact their expected survival through the end of the study participation, and/or impact their ability to comply with the protocol prior/concomitant therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding overall health.

eligibility.text: Females who are pregnant or breastfeeding
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding pregnancy and breastfeeding.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.text: Number of Participants With Adverse Events (AEs).
endpoints.timeframe: AEs were collected from the start of intervention through 30 days post last day of study drug taken. Participants were monitored/assessed for adverse events for a maximum of 17 months which includes a maximum duration of 16 months on treatment.

endpoints.text: Number of Participants With Dose Limiting Toxicities (DLTs)

pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (EGFR, KRAS, PTPN11) but could not map indication "non-small cell lung cancer" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.

relaxations.description: Decrease minimum age from 18 to 13 years
